notes: agricola = subject (nominative, 1st decl.); puellam = object (accusative, 1st decl.); amat = 3rd person singular present
servus = subject (nominative, 2nd decl.); aquam = object (accusative, 1st decl.); portat = 3rd person singular present
dominus = subject (nominative, 2nd decl.); filium = object (accusative, 2nd decl.); vocat = 3rd person singular present
milites = subject (nominative plural, 3rd decl.); pugnant = 3rd person plural present
puella = subject (nominative, 1st decl.); equum = object (accusative, 2nd decl.); amat = 3rd person singular present
nautae = subject (nominative plural, 1st decl.); cibum = object (accusative, 2nd decl.); portabant = 3rd person plural imperfect

english: The king was watching the soldiers.
notes: rex = subject (nominative, 3rd decl.); milites = object (accusative plural, 3rd decl.); spectabat = 3rd person singular imperfect

english: The father was praising the daughter.
notes: pater = subject (nominative, 3rd decl.); filiam = object (accusative, 1st decl.); laudabat = 3rd person singular imperfect

english: The slave was working in the field.
notes: servus = subject (nominative); in agro = prepositional phrase (in + ablative); laborabat = 3rd person singular imperfect
dominus = subject (nominative); servum = object (accusative); necavit = 3rd person singular perfect
puer = subject (nominative, 2nd decl.); patrem = object (accusative, 3rd decl.); vocavit = 3rd person singular perfect
milites = subject (nominative plural, 3rd decl.); oppidum = object (accusative, 2nd decl. neuter); viderunt = 3rd person plural perfect

english: The good farmer praised the slave.
notes: bonus agricola = subject (nominative, adjective agrees with noun); servum = object (accusative); laudavit = 3rd person singular perfect

english: The soldiers were leading the king to the camp.
notes: milites = subject (nominative plural); regem = object (accusative, 3rd decl.); ad castra = to the camp (ad + accusative); ducebant = 3rd person plural imperfect

english: The king was in the camp.
notes: rex = subject (nominative, 3rd decl.); in castris = in the camp (in + ablative plural); erat = 3rd person singular imperfect of esse
